excerpts.text: Admin 1 code has to be 7 digits long. It cannot be the same number seven times and it cannot be sequential such as 1234567 or 7654321.
excerpts.sourcePath: Instructor Led Sections/THE BASICS OF PROGRAMMING/SELF NOTES/Code requirments.txt

excerpts.text: Function keys are records of macros. Hold the function key, enter installer code, press 1 to start recording, perform the disable or enable actions, then hold the same key again and press 1 to end F-key recording.
excerpts.sourcePath: Instructor Led Sections/THE BASICS OF PROGRAMMING/SELF NOTES/Function keys.txt

excerpts.text: Function latch during reset.
excerpts.sourcePath: Instructor Led Sections/THE BASICS OF PROGRAMMING/SELF NOTES/Function keys.txt

excerpts.text: JumpStart is a quick way to add all devices. It is incremental and builds on top of existing programming. It will remove devices that are no longer present and all inputs are assigned to zone 1.
excerpts.sourcePath: Instructor Led Sections/THE BASICS OF PROGRAMMING/SELF NOTES/JUMPSTART.txt

excerpts.text: Must choose SLC protocol on launch. JumpStart is only through annunciator.
excerpts.sourcePath: Instructor Led Sections/THE BASICS OF PROGRAMMING/SELF NOTES/JUMPSTART.txt

excerpts.text: Input point is a single addressable device. Input zone is a collection of input points. All input points must be assigned to one and only one zone.
excerpts.sourcePath: Instructor Led Sections/THE BASICS OF PROGRAMMING/SELF NOTES/Inputs.txt

excerpts.text: Output points are output devices such as circuits or relays. Output points must be assigned to an output group. An output point can be in one and only one output group. One output pattern per output group.
excerpts.sourcePath: Instructor Led Sections/THE BASICS OF PROGRAMMING/SELF NOTES/outputs.txt

excerpts.text: Input zone can be mapped to any number of output groups, and any number of input zones can be mapped to one output group.
excerpts.sourcePath: Instructor Led Sections/THE BASICS OF PROGRAMMING/SELF NOTES/Mapping.txt

excerpts.text: On main panel the SLC is 97. I/O circuit has no letter for point so 98:001. SD protocol uses point ID directly instead of sensor or module letters.
excerpts.sourcePath: Instructor Led Sections/THE BASICS OF PROGRAMMING/SELF NOTES/Point ID.txt

excerpts.text: Cannot program mapping on an annunciator. Can program point types and points on annunciator. Can program the function key through the annunciator.
excerpts.sourcePath: Instructor Led Sections/THE BASICS OF PROGRAMMING/SELF NOTES/Programming.txt

excerpts.text: SLC Device Locator is used to locate a device on the SLC loop. Multiple Device Locator can locate up to 8 devices and can help identify double addresses. I/O Point Control toggles output points or trips input devices and has no timer while using it.
excerpts.sourcePath: Instructor Led Sections/THE BASICS OF PROGRAMMING/SELF NOTES/New Text Document.txt

excerpts.text: For panels previous to version seven, set the panel ID, power up the panel, enter installer code 123456, do not press Enter after typing the password, then go to panel programming and restore defaults.
For version seven or higher, press Enter to open the login menu, select Admin 1, enter the default seven zeros, press Enter, then choose Restore Defaults and confirm yes twice.
Upon startup of your panel, you will receive default password faults and must change Installer, Multi Site Installer, Computer Access and on firmware version 7 and onward the Admin 1 code as well.
excerpts.sourcePath: Instructor Led Sections/THE BASICS OF PROGRAMMING/Covered Slides/3-IFP Programming Presentation - V2.pdf

excerpts.text: On the 10th incorrect guess, the panel locks out the menu for 30 seconds and later requires a support process with an encrypted code and agreement to unlock the panel.
excerpts.sourcePath: Instructor Led Sections/THE BASICS OF PROGRAMMING/Covered Slides/3-IFP Programming Presentation - V2.pdf

excerpts.text: Best practice is to only have one program open at one time. New panels only have USB.
excerpts.sourcePath: Instructor Led Sections/HFSS SOFTWARE/SELF NOTES/General notes.txt

excerpts.text: Detector status reports display values for all detectors, indicate status such as Normal, Maintenance Alert, and Cal Trouble, and can be sorted or exported.
excerpts.sourcePath: Instructor Led Sections/HFSS SOFTWARE/SELF NOTES/Detector status reports.txt

excerpts.text: Event history can be pulled later if you need to check something and event history has filters.
excerpts.sourcePath: Instructor Led Sections/HFSS SOFTWARE/SELF NOTES/Event History.txt

excerpts.text: Latching checked means the output remains true until a manual reset. The notes warn not to have both latching and silenceable selected at the same time. Only one output pattern can be selected per output group.
excerpts.sourcePath: Instructor Led Sections/HFSS SOFTWARE/SELF NOTES/Mapping Charateristics.txt

excerpts.text: Panel password changing through HFSS is from version 8.0.21.10 and only possible with panels using firmware 7.00.16 or higher. Get Panel Passwords reads the existing values for programmed profiles.
excerpts.sourcePath: Instructor Led Sections/HFSS SOFTWARE/SELF NOTES/Changing Password.txt

excerpts.text: The laptop requirements called out in class were a 64-bit Windows 10 or Windows 11 system with at least one active USB port.
The instructor recommended keeping only a single program open at a time because after long programming sessions it becomes easy to edit the wrong panel accidentally.
CLSS Licensing is the HFSS menu path used to log in to the CLSS platform and requires a CLSS account with the proper training and programming license.
excerpts.sourcePath: Instructor Led Sections/HFSS SOFTWARE/Covered Slides/4-HFSS Presentation.pdf

excerpts.text: Every panel must have a SK-NIC for multi panel sites. Network refers to panel to panel, not IPv4. Network panels are peer to peer Series Black panels and each must have a unique ID.
RFP2100 is the same as IFP2100 but does not have a built in interface. A 32 x 2100 network equals 67,200 addressable points.
The ACK, DRILL, RESET and F-Macro keys are disabled until a multi-site user access code has been entered and a specific site has been selected.
SK-NIC uses unshielded twisted-pair wiring or fiber optic cable. The notes call out 16-18 AWG copper up to 3000 ft and different loss budgets for single-mode and multi-mode fiber.

Shutdown all panels being networked, set panel IDs, plug the SK-NIC ribbon cable onto the data network pins, wire NIC port to NIC port A to A and B to B, power panels up, then run the network programming flow and Learn Network logic.
The networking presentation states that an IFP-2100, RFP-2100, IFP-300 and IFP-75 can be on the same network and up to 32 panels can be in a network.
Sites can isolate panels from each other. Events from one site are not shared with other sites, which prevents remote reset or silence behavior across site boundaries.

The basic IFP-2100 contains one built-in SLC supporting up to 159 IDP/SK sensors and 159 IDP/SK modules, or 127 SD devices per loop. Using 6815 or 5815XL expansion increases overall point capacity into the 2100-class range.

The IFP-2100 has eight onboard Flexput circuits configurable for auxiliary power, notification outputs, or conventional smoke detector inputs, plus built-in DACT/IP communication options and programmable relays.

The IFP-300 has one built-in SLC supporting 159 IDP/SK sensors and 159 modules or 127 SD devices per loop, with maximum point capacity of 300 points using 6815 or 254 points using 5815XL for SD devices.
The SK-NIC is the Network Interface Card used on Farenhyt Series panels. It comes standard with copper network connections and supports remote network connectivity options through accessory paths.
The 6815 is an SLC expander for Farenhyt IFP-300/ECS or IFP-2100/ECS analog/addressable panels and increases addressable point capacity on supported systems.

The IFP-2100ECS is an intelligent analog/addressable fire alarm control panel with Emergency Communication System capability. The datasheet references support for amplifier families such as ECS-50W, ECS-125W, ECS-INT50W and ECS-DUAL50W.

The installation manual references support for up to 16 SBUS addressable amplifiers using a combination of ECS-50W, ECS-125W, ECS-INT50W or ECS-DUAL50W for a maximum of 2000 watts per system and up to 128 mappable speaker circuits.
The IFP-300ECS manual references support for up to eight ECS-50W, ECS-125W, ECS-INT50W or ECS-DUAL50W addressable amplifiers, showing the smaller expansion scale compared with the 2100ECS platform.
The ECS-related product references identify ECS-NVCM as the Network Voice Control Module and ECS-LOC as the Local Operator Console family.

The ECS-INT50W is capable of producing 50 watts of audio power on one speaker circuit and is designed to mount in ECS-capable control panels rather than requiring a separate external cabinet in the same way a standalone amp might.
The instructor said to pay attention to the basics that are useful to your learning path, especially points, zones, groups and mapping, instead of trying to become an HFSS expert in one session.

The course outcome described in the project brief is successful completion of three practical lab exercises plus a multiple-choice final exam administered outside the lecture, leading to a one-year factory certification.
